ab: A malalitemuh haw kita anucila i herek nu lahuk?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: Hayi, icuwa saw kita a malalitemuh?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: Idengay haw kita i cacudadan a malalitemuh? A miluing kita.
ch: W:我們約在學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: Kanca tayra i taluan kita, a silusid tu kaku.
ch: M:那我們去聚會所好了，我也會穿族服去.
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: Savangcal saan a macerak ku sakula i niyaru’ niyam. I kacerakan nu varu iri, tayni han tu haw!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: Tala han tu tu nikacanglahan, a mautang tu!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: I tiya han tu i nikalalikidan a tayni isaw!
ch: W: 那你【豐年祭】的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: Hayi, tala han tu nu maku.
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: Kiya mangalay kisu a taluma’ i niyaru’?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: A taluma’ kaku itila a pasevana’ tu micudaday.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: Kiya caay ka itila i tukay a pasevana’ tu micudaday?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: Maurun tu kaku tu tadaniyaru’ nu maku, mangalay kaku a pananukas tu niyaru’.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: U mavana’:ay kisu a mitenun, nacima saw ku pasevana’ay itisuwan?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: Ci vayi nu maku, u savana’ay a mitenun nu niyaru’ cira.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: Kiya mangalay kisu a minanam a mitenun?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: U nu mita a serangawan ku mitenun hananay, hangay, madutuc tu ku serangawan nu mita sa kaku.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: Alesu’ kina epah.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: U nisaepahan nu maku.
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: U mavana’ay kisu haw! A matenes haw a misaepah?
ch: M:你真厲害啊!這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: Caay tu, cacay a lalipayan, kiya madengaay tu. A u panay atu velac ku sapisahemay, tiya palamlam han tu tu tamud a mikiwakiw, ta, pinaru han tu i kureng.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
